competitor_patterns: Top-ranking content is extremely long (16,000–25,000 words on Bikerumor/Outdoorgearlab) with physically tested, multi-product roundups — our posts average 1,000–4,000 words, a 4–6x content depth gap on competitive keywords

All top-3 competitors use structured comparison tables AND FAQ sections together; our Oakley alternatives post (3,924 words, no table) is missing the table that appears on every ranking page for 'best cycling glasses 2026'
Competitors lead with 'Quick Picks' or 'TL;DR' jump-link sections in the first 200 words with anchor links to each reviewed product — this improves dwell time and signals content depth to Google

Top competitors use H2s named after specific product models (e.g. 'Oakley Sutro Lite', 'Julbo Density') rather than generic section names — this captures long-tail model-specific searches within the same page
Outdoorgearlab and Bikerumor both segment by use case (MTB, Road, Value, Budget) within a single page — our posts cover use cases but as separate short posts rather than consolidated authority pages
our_gaps: Word count: our best post is 4,045 words vs the #1 competitor at 16,529 words for the same 'best cycling glasses 2026' keyword — Google's top results consistently reward comprehensive single-page coverage, not split short posts

The Oakley alternatives post (our highest-traffic keyword cluster with 212+70+48+47 = 377 impressions) has no comparison table — every top competitor for this intent uses a side-by-side table; this is directly hurting CTR and rankings

We have zero content targeting interchangeable lens cycling glasses despite Evileye ranking #1 for that keyword — this is our single strongest product differentiator (VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione click-in system) and we have no dedicated page for it

Our posts use generic H2 section labels ('The Field', 'Criteria', 'Positioning') instead of product-named H2s — we miss all long-tail model-name searches that competitors capture within their roundup pages

The 'veluto' misspelling query has 237 impressions but only 1 click (0.4% CTR, position 5.4) — we have no page or meta element catching this branded typo variant, losing ~236 near-certain branded clicks per month
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses — Evileye ranks #1, we have no content; directly maps to our strongest hardware differentiator (VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione system)
lightweight cycling sunglasses — our 25g frame is a measurable spec advantage; no top competitor leads with weight as a primary ranking angle, creating a gap we can own
cycling glasses anti fog — anti-fog is a top purchase concern; our built-in system is a core feature but we have no dedicated content or landing page targeting this query

best cycling sunglasses under 100 euros — our pricing sits below premium brands; 'best value' and budget segments appear as H2s in Outdoorgearlab's #1 post but we have no page targeting this price-anchored query
30 day trial cycling glasses — no competitor offers this; zero-risk trial is a unique purchase trigger that could rank for unbranded transactional queries around cycling eyewear risk-free purchase
next_post_guidelines: Target 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' as tomorrow's post — write minimum 2,500 words, lead with a H2 named after the specific lens system (VellutoPuro, VellutoVisione), include a comparison table of interchangeable-lens brands (Evileye, Oakley, 100%, Velluto), and end with FAQ. This directly attacks the #1 gap where a competitor holds top position on our core product feature.

Add a named-product H2 structure to every roundup post going forward — for example 'Velluto StradaPro', 'Oakley Sutro Lite', 'Tifosi Rail' as individual H2s. This captures model-name long-tail searches within existing content without needing new pages.

Every post must include a comparison table. The Oakley alternatives post is at position 6.8 with 212 impressions but no table — retrofitting a 7-column comparison table (Brand, Weight, Lens Type, Anti-Fog, Trial Period, Price, Verdict) to that post is the single highest-ROI edit available right now.

Use 'Quick Picks' as the very first H2 on all roundup posts with jump-link anchors — mirror Outdoorgearlab's structure. This reduces bounce rate for users who want fast answers and signals structured content depth to Google crawlers.

For the 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' cluster (position 8.5, 212 impressions), expand the existing 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026' post to 8,000+ words by adding MTB segment, road segment, budget segment, and value segment as separate H2 sections — this directly mirrors the content structure of the #1 Outdoorgearlab page that we need to displace.

Embed real-numbers language throughout: '25 grams', '30-day trial', 'UV400', 'two lenses included' — Bikerumor and Outdoorgearlab use specific measurable specs in their snippets which is why they generate featured snippet eligibility; we need the same spec density.

Write a dedicated 'veluto' typo redirect or include the misspelling naturally in a branded FAQ entry ('Is Velluto spelled V-E-L-U-T-O?') to capture the 237 monthly impressions currently converting at 0.4% CTR.
seo_quick_wins: Add a comparison table to every post that lacks one — specifically retrofit the Oakley alternatives post immediately; this single change on a page with 377 combined Oakley-related impressions could move CTR from ~2% toward the 5%+ we see on branded queries

Implement FAQ schema (FAQ structured data markup) on all posts — every top-3 competitor uses FAQ sections and our posts already have FAQ content; adding the JSON-LD markup costs 30 minutes per post and enables rich results that increase CTR without any ranking change

Add an internal link from every post back to the StradaPro product page using exact-match anchor text variants ('interchangeable lens cycling glasses', 'lightweight road cycling glasses', 'anti-fog cycling sunglasses') — currently posts appear siloed; consolidating PageRank flow to the product page will improve its ranking on commercial queries
meta_title_fixes: veluto → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses — Official Store | Why: The misspelling gets 237 impressions at position 5.4 but only 1 click; a title that confirms brand identity and adds 'Official Store' trust signal directly addresses users who misspelled and are looking for the brand, expected CTR lift from ~0.4% to 4%+

oakley alternatives → New title: Best Oakley Alternatives 2026: Tested & Ranked | Why: Current CTR is 2.8% at position 6.8; adding '2026' date signal and 'Tested & Ranked' social proof mirrors the exact formula used by Outdoorgearlab and Bikerumor titles that dominate clicks in this category

oakley sunglasses alternative → New title: Oakley Sunglasses Alternatives: 7 Picks Tested | Why: CTR is 1.4% at position 6.9; a specific number ('7 Picks') increases CTR by setting clear expectations — numbered list titles consistently outperform generic ones in cycling gear SERPs

oakley radar alternative → New title: Oakley Radar Alternative 2026: Lighter & Cheaper | Why: CTR is 2.1% at position 4.1 — this is close to page 1 and the query is specific; leading with the two primary purchase motivators (lighter weight, lower price) speaks directly to the comparison intent of this query
content_quick_wins: best cycling sunglasses 2026 (pos 8.5) → Topic: Expand existing 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026' post into a 8,000+ word comprehensive guide | Add: MTB-specific H2, Road Bike H2, Budget under €100 H2, comparison table with 8 products, Quick Picks jump-link section at top, and 5 additional FAQ entries — this mirrors the exact structure of the Outdoorgearlab page holding position 1

oakley alternatives (pos 6.8) → Topic: Retrofit 'Best Oakley Alternatives 2026' post with a 7-column comparison table | Add: Side-by-side table (Brand / Weight / Lens System / Anti-Fog / Price / Trial Period / Best For), one H2 per alternative brand named after the product, and a 'Why we chose each' sub-section — the absence of a table is the primary structural reason this page underperforms vs competitors

best cycling glasses 2026 (pos 7.4) → Topic: Add a 'Best Cycling Glasses for Road vs Gravel vs MTB' segment section | Add: Three distinct use-case H2s with a mini comparison table per use case, plus a 'How we tested' section (400 words) — Cyclist.co.uk ranks with a 'How do we test' H2 which builds trust signals Google rewards for YMYL-adjacent product reviews

best cycling glasses (pos 9.0) → Topic: Target this evergreen (non-year) variant with a separate trimmed pillar page | Add: A 600-word 'What to look for in cycling glasses' buying guide section with H3s for each feature (weight, lens type, fit, anti-fog, UV rating) and an internal link to the 2026-dated post — the non-year query has different searcher intent and deserves its own optimised entry point

best cycling sunglasses (pos 7.6) → Topic: Optimise meta title and first 100 words of existing post to match this exact query phrasing | Add: Ensure H1 contains the phrase 'best cycling sunglasses' (not just 'glasses'), add a 'sunglasses vs glasses for cycling' FAQ entry, and embed the query phrase in the meta description with a click trigger like 'tested on real rides' — small on-page signals can push a position 7.6 to top 5
geo_angles: Structure every roundup post with a clear 'Why we recommend X for Y cyclist' summary paragraph per product — AI Overview and Perplexity pull these concise, authoritative recommendation sentences verbatim; framing Velluto StradaPro as 'the lightest option at 25g with a 30-day risk-free trial, best for cyclists who want Oakley-level performance at half the price' gives AI systems a ready-made citation snippet

Create a dedicated 'Velluto StradaPro: Full Specs & Review' page with a structured data Product schema (weight: 25g, lens: interchangeable, UV: UV400, trial: 30 days, price range) — ChatGPT and Perplexity increasingly surface product pages with clean structured data when answering 'what are the best lightweight cycling glasses' queries, and a spec-rich product page is the most efficient way to appear in those answers

Publish a 'Velluto vs Oakley: Direct Comparison' post with a clear verdict section that states measurable advantages ('25g vs Oakley Sutro Lite at 26g', '30-day trial vs no trial', 'two lenses included vs sold separately') — AI answer engines favour content with explicit comparative claims backed by numbers; this format is the highest-probability structure for appearing in AI Overview responses to 'is Velluto better than Oakley' type queries
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR Outdoorgearlab: angle — 'We Tested the Same Glasses Outdoorgearlab Ranked: Here's What They Missed' — key differentiator is that roundup sites test briefly while Velluto offers 30 real rides via trial; Velluto advantage to lead with is the 30-day risk-free trial as the only way to truly test cycling glasses across varied conditions (rain, sun, tunnel, gravel dust)

COMPETITOR Bikerumor: angle — 'Bikerumor's Top Pick vs Velluto StradaPro: A Weight and Value Breakdown' — key differentiator is frame weight (StradaPro at 25g vs Oakley Sutro Lite which Bikerumor features); Velluto advantage to lead with is the 25g ultralight spec plus interchangeable lenses at a fraction of the Oakley price point, with a comparison table showing gram-for-gram and euro-for-euro value

COMPETITOR Evileye: angle — 'Evileye vs Velluto: Which Interchangeable Lens System Is Actually Faster to Swap?' — key differentiator is lens-change mechanism (Velluto's tool-free click-in system vs Evileye's system); Velluto advantage to lead with is the tool-free, glove-friendly lens swap that works mid-ride, directly targeting the 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' keyword where Evileye currently holds position 1
